articles.title: 'Invisible mouse' made a mess of PC rebuild
articles.snippet: You can't fix what you can't see – especially when your workspace is a maelstrom Who, Me?  Welcome to yet another Monday, and therefore to this week's edition of Who, Me? For those unfamiliar, it's The Register's reader-contributed column that shares your stories of workplace messes, and how you tried to clean them up without dirtying your career prospects.…
articles.ai_summary: An article discusses a reader's chaotic experience while attempting to rebuild a PC, highlighting the challenges of managing a messy workspace. It illustrates the difficulties of fixing problems without clear visibility.
articles.why_it_matters: Emphasizes the importance of organization in tech tasks.

articles.title: NASA working on ‘Big Bang’ upgrade to keep the Voyagers alive for longer
articles.snippet: Tests scheduled for May can’t come soon enough after VGER 1 power glitch led to instrument shutdown NASA has revealed it’s working on a plan called “The Big Bang” that it hopes will extend the working lives of the Voyager probes.…
articles.ai_summary: NASA is developing a plan called 'The Big Bang' to extend the operational life of the Voyager probes after a recent power glitch. This upgrade is crucial for maintaining communication with these historic spacecraft.
articles.why_it_matters: Highlights ongoing efforts to preserve valuable space exploration missions.

articles.title: Indonesia’s game rating system paused amid claims it leaked developer creds and glimpses of major new titles
articles.snippet: PLUS: India bins ID app pre-install plan; Robot wins Beijing half-marathon; AI writing Manga speech bubbles; and more! Asia In Brief  Indonesia’s Ministry of Communication and Digital Affairs has suspended the nation’s game rating system (IGRS) after claims the service leaked developer creds and video of unreleased games.…

articles.title: Mozilla’s Thunderbolt Is an Open-Source AI Client, but What Exactly Is It?
articles.snippet:
Mozilla's Thunderbolt is not a model or chatbot, but an open-source AI client built to give organizations more control over models, data, and workflows.
The post Mozilla’s Thunderbolt Is an Open-Source AI Client, but What Exactly Is It? appeared first on Hong…
articles.ai_summary: Mozilla has introduced Thunderbolt, an open-source AI client designed to give organizations greater control over their AI models and workflows. This tool aims to enhance transparency and customization in AI usage.
articles.why_it_matters: Promotes open-source solutions in AI, fostering innovation and control.
